channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Adam Driver declines to comment on Lena Dunham's on-set behaviour claims during Cannes press conference
item.description: At a press conference in Cannes for his new film Paper Tiger, actor Adam Driver avoided addressing allegations made by his former Girls co-star Lena Dunham in her recent memoir Famesick. Dunham, who c...

item.title: 90s rave-themed World Cup watch parties announced for London venues this summer
item.description: Promoter Kick Off Club has announced a series of 90s rave-themed screenings for the 2026 World Cup in London, offering football fans an alternative to watching the late-night matches at home. The tour...

item.title: Remembering Tatiana Samoilova, Icon of Soviet Cinema
item.description: Tatiana Samoilova was a defining figure in Soviet cinema, celebrated for her emotionally rich performances and timeless beauty. Rising to international fame with her role in the 1957 classic 'The Cran...

item.title: Kyle Schwarber Hits 20 Home Runs Early in 2026, Showing Historic Power
item.description: Philadelphia Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber is off to a historic start in the 2026 MLB season. Through his first 46 games, Schwarber has already hit 20 home runs, putting him on pace for an unprecede...

item.title: Capetonians and South Africans rally behind 19-year-old Creashy Maart in her battle with terminal cancer
item.description: Nineteen-year-old Creashy Maart from Mitchells Plain in Cape Town has captured the hearts of South Africans after receiving a terminal cancer diagnosis. Diagnosed with lung problems as a tiny baby, sh...

item.title: Australian startup founders respond to proposed capital gains tax changes with AI images
item.description: Several Australian tech founders have responded to the federal government's proposed changes to the capital gains tax (CGT) by posting AI-generated images of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in their w...

item.title: Israel PM claims progress in targeting those responsible for October 7 attacks
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has declared that Israel is close to neutralizing all individuals responsible for the October 7 attacks. The statement comes amid ongoing conflict between Isr...

item.title: Generation X Band Photographed by Adrian Boot in West London, 1982
item.description: In 1982, renowned British photographer Adrian Boot captured the punk and new wave band Generation X during a professional photo session in West London. Boot, celebrated for his iconic images of the Br...

item.title: 'Michael' reclaims number one spot at North American box office
item.description: The film 'Michael,' inspired by the life and legacy of the King of Pop, Michael Jackson, has impressively returned to the top spot of the North American box office. Audiences have shown renewed enthus...

item.title: How a Taxi Driver Helped Me Escape a Dangerous Situation
item.description: In 1980s Sydney, the suburb of Darlinghurst was a mix of creative nightlife and dangerous streets, with frequent drug-related incidents. A 23-year-old woman, part of the local underground scene, was w...

item.title: Man in early 30s dies after stabbing in Etobicoke apartment, suspect arrested at scene
item.description: Toronto Police responded to a fatal stabbing in an apartment building in the Rexdale neighbourhood of Etobicoke early Sunday morning. Officers were called to the area of Albion Road and Kipling Avenue...

item.title: Libyan National Army Conducts Large-Scale Military Drills to Showcase Strength and Unity
item.description: The Libyan National Army (LNA), led by Marshal Khalifa Haftar and controlling eastern and southern Libya, is conducting its largest military exercises to date. The drills, involving over 25,000 troops...

item.title: ICT CS Kabogo Rejects Treasury Plan to Privatisation KBC and Posta Kenya
item.description: Information, Communications and Digital Economy Cabinet Secretary William Kabogo has publicly opposed the National Treasury's plan to privatise two major state corporations: Kenya Broadcasting Corpora...

item.title: Xabi Alonso appointed Chelsea manager to rebuild after trophy-less season
item.description: Chelsea have confirmed Xabi Alonso as their new manager on a four-year deal starting this July, aiming to restore stability following a turbulent season. The 44-year-old Spaniard steps in after Chelse...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns secure 1-0 first-leg lead against AS FAR in CAF Champions League final
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns claimed a narrow 1-0 victory over AS FAR Rabat in the first leg of the CAF Champions League final at Loftus Versfeld on Sunday. Aubrey Modiba scored the decisive goal with a brillian...

item.title: DSWD distributes P220 million cash aid to more than 45,000 TNC drivers in Metro Manila
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) has successfully distributed over P220 million in cash assistance to more than 45,000 drivers from transport network companies (TNCs) such as Gr...

item.title: Hollywood Actresses in Shamrock-Themed Dresses: Vintage St. Patrick’s Day Publicity Photos from the 1940s
item.description: In the 1940s, Hollywood studios frequently produced holiday-themed promotional photographs of their actresses as a way to maintain public interest and generate festive publicity. For St. Patrick’s Day...

item.title: Two Key Aspirants Pull Out from Kebbi APC Senatorial Primaries Citing Party Dynamics
item.description: Two big aspirants wey don pull out from di All Progressives Congress (APC) senatorial primaries for Kebbi State. Di primaries suppose hold on May 18, 2026. Senator Bala Ibn Na’All, wey represent Kebbi...

item.title: Greek Islands Struggle with Overtourism and Overdevelopment
item.description: Popular Greek islands such as Santorini and Mykonos are facing severe overtourism, straining both the environment and local communities. Santorini, known for its iconic cliffside villages and blue-dom...

item.title: Taiwan’s President Urges Continued U.S. Arms Support Amid China Tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te has firmly stated that Taiwan will never be 'traded away' following remarks from former U.S. President Donald Trump suggesting arms sales to Taiwan could be used as a ...
